$statusLegend.ported: byte-equivalent to upstream (CI fails on drift)
$statusLegend.adapted: TypeScript port and/or namespace swap; logically equivalent
$statusLegend.extended: fork adds behavior on top of upstream
$statusLegend.added: file unique to the fork (no upstream)

files.notes: name + textdomain swapped to the artisanpack namespace; attribute schema, supports, usesContext/providesContext carried verbatim for parity.

Delegates to the registered core/navigation edit (forked-block cutover keeps it registered) so the fork inherits the upstream + V1 editor surface and the same @wordpress/core-data shim selectors. Re-porting was rejected to avoid regressing the V1 nav-editor / template-part editing surfaces (#413 acceptance criterion).
Delegates to the registered core/navigation save so stored inner-block markup stays byte-identical.
Bidirectional core/navigation ↔ artisanpack/navigation block transforms for the rollout window.
Inline SVG inserter icon (editor lacks dashicons.css).
Adapted to the visual-editor auto-discovery contract.

knownDivergences: edit.tsx delegates to the registered core/navigation edit rather than re-porting upstream edit.js — chosen to inherit the V1 editor surface unchanged and read entity data through the same core-data shim selectors.
navigation is server-rendered: the front-end markup is produced by the Blade/React/Vue renderers from stamped _resolved* attributes, not by save().
transforms.ts adds bidirectional block transforms that do not exist upstream — required to coexist with core/navigation during the V1 rollout.

Upstream deprecated.js (the multi-version migrateIdToRef / migrateWithLayout / typography-preset chain) is NOT ported: the fork ships inside 1.0.0 before any host persists artisanpack/navigation markup (plan 13 §2.1 — no migration window), so the only legacy path is pasted core/navigation markup, which the core/navigation ↔ artisanpack/navigation transform handles. Revisit if a host ever persists a pre-fork artisanpack/navigation shape.
